title: Euler classes transgressions and Eistenstein cohomology of GL(N)
speaker: Nicolas Bergeron
abstract: Abstract: In work-in-progress with Pierre Charollois, Luis Garcia and Akshay Venkatesh we give a new construction of some Eisenstein classes for $GL_N (Z)$ that were first considered by Nori and Sczech. The starting point of this construction is a theorem of Sullivan on the vanishing of the Euler class of $SL_N$ (Z)-vector bundles and the explicit transgression of this Euler class by Bismut and Cheeger. Their proof indeed produces a universal form that can be thought of as a kernel for a regularized theta lift for the reductive dual pair $(GL_1 , GL_N )$.

proof_stages.label: Euler and Thom classes for SL(N,Z)-bundles
proof_stages.summary: The talk starts from Sullivan's theorem that the Euler class of an SL(N,Z) vector bundle is rationally zero, reviews Thom and Euler classes, and reformulates an SL(N,Z)-bundle as a vector bundle with a preserved lattice and hence an associated torus bundle.

proof_stages.label: Sullivan torsion-point vanishing lemma
proof_stages.summary: For the torus bundle, Sullivan's key observation is proved: the cohomology class of the m-torsion locus minus m^n times the zero section vanishes rationally, using the pull-push relations for multiplication by m on the torus fibers.

proof_stages.label: From rational vanishing to canonical linking and Eisenstein classes
proof_stages.summary: The torsion-point vanishing is used to deduce rational vanishing of the Euler class, then the long exact sequence for the complement of the torsion locus, together with Faltings/Lieberman's splitting trick, produces canonical lifts Z_m whose torsion-section pullbacks define Eisenstein classes and linking numbers.

proof_stages.label: Mathai-Quillen analytic transgression
proof_stages.summary: Bismut-Cheeger's analytic refinement is recast through Mathai-Quillen formalism, giving a canonical equivariant universal Thom form phi and a Schwartz-valued transgression form psi on the universal GL_N(R)/SO_N vector bundle, with scaling by the central GL(1) selecting the primitive.

proof_stages.label: Theta series and Eisenstein differential forms
proof_stages.summary: The Schwartz-valued transgression psi is summed over a lattice to form theta series on the universal torus bundle, Mellin transformed and evaluated at s=0 to obtain closed Eisenstein forms representing the canonical transgressions of degree-zero torsion divisors.

proof_stages.label: Cohomology classes and toric periods for GL(N)
proof_stages.summary: After identifying the torsion-section pullbacks with the Eisenstein classes of Nori and Szech, the speaker pairs them with cycles from totally real degree-n extensions, where functoriality reduces the computation to line bundles and yields classical L-values and rationality/integrality statements.

proof_stages.label: Seesaw extensions and future cohomological framework
proof_stages.summary: The toric-period computation is reframed as a GL(1)-GL(N) seesaw, suggesting new dual-pair constructions such as GL(2)-GL(2n), relations among products of Eisenstein series, connections to Charollois cocycles, and a possible need for link or motivic cohomology beyond the classical Jacobi-form setting.

examples.name: Totally real field torus period
examples.note: Pairing the Eisenstein class with the torus attached to a totally real degree-n extension recovers L-values and the Klingen-Siegel theorem, and this example is explicitly used to motivate the later seesaw viewpoint.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker pairs the Eisenstein class with the torus from a totally real field, uses the splitting into line bundles, and says the computation gives the associated L-value and integrality statement
